design_philosophy.core_loop: Race against AI opponents across multiple laps, destroy competitor vehicles for monetary rewards, use earnings to upgrade car components and weapons between races, accumulate points to advance through divisions and planets
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing aggressive vehicle combat against maintaining position in the race; managing limited weapon/turbo capacity that resets each lap; deciding when to attack vs. focus on lap placement
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: High-speed vehicular combat racing with rock music as backdrop; destruction without permanent consequences; progression through increasingly challenging planets with customizable racing machines
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses to be pure racing sim—weaponized destruction is core mechanic with explicit monetary rewards for eliminating competitors. Emphasizes arcade spectacle over simulation. Includes commentator hype and comic-book aesthetic. Punishment for failure is repeating a division, not game over, encouraging persistence

difficulty.lives_system: No traditional lives system; cars respawn after destruction with full health within seconds
difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows resumption from any season start; dropped players in two-player mode can rejoin via password
difficulty.checkpointing: Season-based progression; incomplete point totals reset division but preserve money and vehicle purchases
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can purchase upgraded engines, tires, shocks, shields, and weapons between races to improve competitiveness; weapon/turbo capacity upgradeable up to seven levels; can choose between vehicle models (with tradeoff of losing all upgrades)

progression.upgrade_system: Between-race monetary economy: earn money from race placement (1st-3rd) and attack/lapping bonuses; purchase vehicle component upgrades (engine, tires, shocks, shielding), weapon capacity expansions (frontal/rear/turbo), and vehicle model replacements
progression.unlock_mechanism: Points-based advancement; must achieve sufficient points during each planet division's racing season to progress to next division or next planet; two divisions per planet
replayability_hooks: Password system enables save states across sessions
Multiplayer competitive racing with asymmetric dropouts
Multiple vehicle models with distinct upgrade implications
Planet/track variety with environmental hazards (mines, oil slicks, snow, lava)

world_building_techniques: Comic-book inspired character designs (humans and aliens)
Planet-specific track environments (varying hazards and aesthetics)
Licensed rock/heavy metal soundtrack as cultural anchor
Commentator 'Loudmouth Larry' provides race play-by-play and flavor
Character progression across divisions creates implicit tournament narrative

faction_indicators: Recurring AI rivals 'Rip' and 'Shred' appear in all races (implicit antagonists)
Planet-specific unique AI opponent per race in single-player
Two-player mode enables human vs. human rivalry with asymmetric progression (leading player can abandon trailing player)
danger_signals: Weapon systems (forward/rear attacks) allow player-initiated destruction of opponents
Attack bonus rewards explicitly incentivize eliminating competitors
Two-player dropout mechanic creates potential social friction when advancing player chooses to leave loser behind
environmental_storytelling: Track hazards (mines, oil slicks, snow, lava) suggest hostile racing environment
Isometric perspective emphasizes terrain navigation and environmental obstacles
Vehicle respawn mechanic (instant full-health resurrection) suggests consequence-free violence endemic to this racing sport
